findings.message: reported p-value is consistent with the achievable p-range from the statistic (allowing for the statistic's printed precision)

routed_to_human.note: T4 over-reach / dissociation from a null. The headline asserts a SELECTIVE effect — interaction significant 'only for cognitive flexibility' while 'no significant interaction' for inhibitory control or working memory. This infers a difference-between-domains from one significant interaction (switch cost, c26: F(2,41)=5.15, p=.010) plus non-significant ones; a non-significant interaction is not evidence of no interaction (Gelman & Stern: the difference between 'significant' and 'non-significant' is not itself significant). Confirms-it: a direct 3-way (Domain x Group x Time) interaction test, or interaction effect sizes with CIs showing the flexibility interaction is reliably LARGER than the inhibition/WM ones — not merely on the other side of alpha=0.05.
routed_to_human.quote:
significant Group × Time interactions were observed 
only for cognitive flexibility, while no significant interaction effects were found 
for inhibitory control or working memory.

routed_to_human.note: T3 power for a between-group interaction at n=14/14/16. The significant flexibility interaction has eta_p^2=0.20 (f~0.50, well above the f=0.25 the study was powered for, c1), so the POSITIVE result is not underpowered. The risk is the NULLS: the design is powered for main effects, not for a 2-df Group x Time interaction, so 'no interaction' for inhibition/WM may reflect low power rather than a true absence. Confirms-it: a sensitivity analysis reporting the smallest interaction effect detectable at 80% power with this N, and whether the inhibition/WM interaction CIs exclude that value.
routed_to_human.quote:
significant Group × Time interactions were observed 
only for cognitive flexibility, while no significant interaction effects were found 
for inhibitory control or working memory.

routed_to_human.note: T3 underpowered null. The Time x Group interaction is reported non-significant, F(2,41)=2.02, p=.146, but eta_p^2=0.09 corresponds to f~0.31 — a medium-to-large effect dismissed as 'not significant' at ~14/group. Treating this as 'no interaction' (and folding it into the inhibitory-control-is-unaffected story, c28) over-reads an underpowered null. Confirms-it: achieved power for f=0.31 at this N, or a CI on the interaction effect size.
routed_to_human.quote:
The Time × Group interaction was not significant, F(2, 
41) = 2.02, p = 0.146, η2ₚ = 0.09.

routed_to_human.note: T3 power-analysis scope. G*Power was run for a generic 3x2 mixed ANOVA at f=0.25 -> N=42, but the study's headline inference is a Group x Time INTERACTION (numerator df=2), and an omnibus-f=0.25 calculation does not guarantee power for that specific interaction term, nor for the Bonferroni-adjusted simple effects that follow. Achieved analyzed N=44 ~ target, so recruitment met the plan; the open question is whether the plan targeted the right effect. Confirms-it: report the effect (main vs interaction) and the f the 42 was computed for, ideally with the G*Power protocol.
routed_to_human.quote:
Assuming a medium effect size 
(f = 0.25), an alpha level of 0.05, and a statistical power of 0.80, the 
required total sample size was 42 participants (14 per group).

routed_to_human.note: Field-plausibility / framing, not an error. t(26)=14.90, d=5.62 for HIIT vs MIAE mean HR is a MANIPULATION CHECK — it confirms the two intensities produced different heart rates, which is true by design; the colossal d simply reflects tightly-controlled HR bands (173+/-5.5 vs 140+/-6.1). Recomputing d from the Table 1 means/SDs reproduces ~5.7, so the statistic is internally consistent. Flagging only so the report does not let this huge effect size be read as evidence for the cognitive hypothesis. Confirms-it: none needed; this is a note on emphasis.
routed_to_human.quote:
the mean HR in the HIIT group was significantly higher 
than that in the MIAE group, t(26) = 14.90, p < 0.001, Cohen’s 
d = 5.62.

routed_to_human.note: T1/T3 protocol fidelity. HIIT prescribed at 80-90% HRmax and MIAE at 60-70% HRmax for 20 min. Mean achieved HRs (173 / 140 bpm, Table 1) imply HRmax ~192-216 (HIIT) and 200-233 (MIAE) to hit those bands — i.e. the intensities are only verifiable against each participant's measured/estimated HRmax. Plausible for ~19-year-olds (220-age ~ 201) but the paper should state how HRmax was determined. Confirms-it: the HRmax method (measured vs 220-age) and the per-group %HRmax actually achieved.
routed_to_human.quote: participants performed highintensity interval exercise (80–90% HRmax) for 20 min.

routed_to_human.note: T2 minor: 48 recruited (16/group) vs the a-priori 42 (14/group) from c1. Over-recruiting is fine and even prudent against attrition, but the 14-vs-16 final cells (Control retained 16, exercise arms dropped to 14) yield slightly UNEQUAL groups, which mildly affects the mixed-ANOVA's robustness to the homogeneity/sphericity assumptions for the Group factor. Not an error. Confirms-it: a note on whether equality-of-variance / sphericity were checked given the unequal cells.
routed_to_human.quote: A total of 48 participants were recruited (Figure 1).

routed_to_human.note: Whether 'acute exercise selectively benefits cognitive flexibility but not inhibition or working memory' is an important / novel contribution — vs already-established in the acute-exercise literature — is a value judgement LITMUS does not score (DESIGN §3.5). Routed for an editor/domain reviewer.
routed_to_human.quote:
significant Group × Time interactions were observed 
only for cognitive flexibility, while no significant interaction effects were found 
for inhibitory control or working memory.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach found. Within-subjects pre->post (Time) effect or Table-2/Table-1 within-group statistic; correctly described and NOT mis-attributed to a between-group cause. No deterministic verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8); review clears it of claim-vs-evidence over-statement. Numeric recompute deferred to a synthesized T0/T2 verifier.

revealed a significant main effect of Time, F(1, 41) = 13.85, p < 0.001, 
η2ₚ  = 0.25,
